executive_summary.question: How does the BNS define 'criminal conspiracy'?
executive_summary.generated_answer: According to the context chunk 1 [Chunk-ID: 40abf7d3-8696-4424-88ed-e3ba45bfe867], the definition of 'criminal conspiracy' is provided in section 61, which states that when two or more persons agree with the common object to do, or cause to be done, an illegal act or an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy [Chunk-ID: 40abf7d3-8696-4424-88ed-e3ba45bfe867]. Additionally, it is provided that no agreement except an agreement to commit an offence shall amount to a criminal conspiracy unless some act besides the agreement is done by one or more parties to such agreement in pursuance thereof [Chunk-ID: 40abf7d3-8696-4424-88ed-e3ba45bfe867].

evidence_analysis.claim_text: The definition of 'criminal conspiracy' is provided in section 61.
evidence_analysis.supporting_evidence:
Of criminal conspiracy
61. (1) When two or more persons agree with the common object to do, or cause to be
done––
(a) an illegal act; or
(b) an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated
a criminal conspiracy:
Provided that no agreement except an agreement to commit an offence shall amount to
a criminal conspiracy unless some act besides the agreement is done by one or more parties
to such agreement in pursuance thereof.

evidence_analysis.claim_text: A criminal conspiracy is an agreement to do, or cause to be done, an illegal act or an act which is not illegal by illegal means.
evidence_analysis.supporting_evidence:

61. (1) When two or more persons agree with the common object to do, or cause to be
done––
(a) an illegal act; or
(b) an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated
a criminal conspiracy:
Provided that no agreement except an agreement to commit an offence shall amount to
a criminal conspiracy unless some act besides the agreement is done by one or more parties
to such agreement in pursuance thereof.

evidence_analysis.claim_text: An agreement to commit an offence is required for a criminal conspiracy.
evidence_analysis.supporting_evidence:
Of criminal conspiracy
61. (1) When two or more persons agree with the common object to do, or cause to be
done––
(a) an illegal act; or

a criminal conspiracy:
Provided that no agreement except an agreement to commit an offence shall amount to

evidence_analysis.claim_text: Some act besides the agreement must be done by one or more parties to the agreement for it to be a criminal conspiracy.
